input.prompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

input.initialPrompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

data.content:
Here's a MAUI `CollectionView` with single selection and pull-to-refresh:

**XAML:**

```xml
<RefreshView Command="{Binding RefreshCommand}"
             IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}">
    <CollectionView ItemsSource="{Binding Items}"
                    SelectionMode="Single"
                    SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedItem}"
                    SelectionChangedCommand="{Binding SelectionChangedCommand}">
        <CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
            <DataTemplate x:DataType="models:Item">
                <Grid Padding="10">
                    <Label Text="{Binding Name}" FontSize="16" />
                </Grid>
            </DataTemplate>
        </CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
    </CollectionView>
</RefreshView>
```

**ViewModel bindings needed:**

| Property/Command | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `Items` (`ObservableCollection<Item>`) | Data source |
| `SelectedItem` (`Item`) | Tracks selected item; clear it after navigation to allow reselection |
| `SelectionChangedCommand` (`IAsyncRelayCommand`) | Triggers navigation to detail page |
| `IsRefreshing` (`bool`) | Controls the refresh spinner |
| `RefreshCommand` (`IAsyncRelayCommand`) | Calls your API, repopulates `Items`, then sets `IsRefreshing = false` |

**Key ViewModel logic (CommunityToolkit.Mvvm):**

```csharp
[RelayCommand]
async Task SelectionChanged()
{
    if (SelectedItem is null) return;
    await Shell.Current.GoToAsync(nameof(DetailPage), new Dictionary<string, object>
    {
        { "Item", SelectedItem }
    });
    SelectedItem = null; // reset so the same item can be tapped again
}

[RelayCommand]
async Task Refresh()
{
    Items = new ObservableCollection<Item>(await _apiService.GetItemsAsync());
    IsRefreshing = false;
}
```

**Tips:**
- Setting `SelectedItem = null` after navigation allows the same row to be re-selected later.
- `RefreshView` wraps the `CollectionView`—not the other way around.
- Use `SelectionChangedCommand` (bound on `CollectionView`) rather than a `TapGestureRecognizer` to get proper visual selection feedback.
data.reasoningText: The user is asking for a .NET MAUI CollectionView example with single item selection and pull-to-refresh, along with XAML and ViewModel binding explanations. This is a straightforward knowledge question - I can answer directly without needing to search any codebase.
